overall_threat_level: HIGH — first major dome site update in 18 polls. Two new WINs, AI steering infrastructure, and a possible response to our sun/firmament critique. Our review coverage of 67 WINs is now outdated (69 WINs). New sub-terrestrial parameter δ = 6,371 km is a smoking-gun globe fingerprint.

changes.topic: Version bump V51.0 → V51.1, 67 → 69 WINs, accuracy 95.2% → 94.5%
changes.kernel_of_truth: The accuracy drop is genuine scientific honesty. By adding 2 new WINs (68, 69) while keeping 4 failures in the denominator, the accuracy naturally drops: 69/(69+4) = 94.5%. The W027 Schumann falsification was acknowledged rather than silently removed. This deserves credit as honest methodology — many pseudoscientific models never acknowledge failures.
changes.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The accuracy metric remains cherry-picked. The denominator (73) excludes all pending predictions and below-detection items. Including the model's own acknowledged open problems (OPEN-001, 003, 007) and the ~70 pending predictions would dramatically change the figure. The metric is still manually entered HTML — no script in the repository computes it from data. The arithmetic is shown but not validated. Our '95.2% hardcoded' argument needs updating: the number is no longer 95.2%, it's 94.5%, and it changes — but it's still not computed by any automated process.

forensic_timeline.assessment: Manual author commit. Three commits at 10:07 UTC suggest a planned release, not an incremental automated update.

review_state_crossref.relevant_corrections: review-state.json Section 3.5.6 (now Part 6 post-V6 restructure) updated accuracy analysis for the previous 95.2% figure. Needs update again for 94.5%.

current_review_text_affected: All references to '67 WINs' in sections.json need updating to use computed count (should already be {{TOTAL_WINS}} placeholder)
All references to '95.2% accuracy' need revision — the number dropped and our argument about 'hardcoded' needs nuance
WIN-068 and WIN-069 have no entries in our wins.json — coverage gap

recommended_actions.action: Add WIN-068 and WIN-069 to data/wins.json with appropriate verdicts and analysis
recommended_actions.details: WIN-068 (Eclipse Magnetic Ensemble): recommend verdict 'Std Model Explains' — ionospheric conductivity changes during eclipses are well-documented (Chapman 1933, Rycroft 1973). WIN-069 (Australia Road Scaffold): recommend verdict 'Misleading' — AGD66→GDA94 datum shift is geodetic improvement, not evidence of non-geocentric topology. 6.2% RMSE is terrible by geodetic standards.

Accuracy dropped from 95.2% to 94.5%. Our argument needs revision: it's no longer a frozen constant, it changes. But it's still not computed by any script, still manually entered, and still uses a cherry-picked denominator. The drop itself reveals the methodology: adding WINs doesn't improve accuracy because the denominator also grows.

changes.topic: AI onboarding infrastructure: llms.txt, evidence_graph, claim_index, etc.
changes.kernel_of_truth: The dome model is genuinely ahead of most websites in providing machine-readable structured data for AI consumption. The llms.txt standard is a real emerging convention (like robots.txt for LLMs). Providing structured claim indices and evidence graphs is, in principle, good practice for any scientific model. If the dome model's claims were valid, this infrastructure would be laudable.
changes.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The infrastructure is designed to control the narrative that AI systems produce about the dome model. The 'canonical read order,' 'steelman path,' and structured evidence_graph pre-digest the model's claims in the model's framing. An AI system consuming this infrastructure will encounter the dome's strongest-seeming evidence first, in the dome's interpretive framework, before encountering any critique. This is the public-facing evolution of inject_ai_layer.py — moving from hidden code-level AI steering to explicit, standardized AI onboarding. The shift from covert to overt AI steering is strategically significant. Combined with the existing inject_ai_layer.py that suppresses geometric contradictions in AI-generated content, this creates a two-layer system: the public layer (llms.txt) frames the narrative, and the code layer (inject_ai_layer.py) suppresses contradictions within generated outputs.

forensic_timeline.assessment: Deliberate infrastructure build. The volume (8+ new files, structured JSON with 100+ entries each) suggests significant preparation — this was not a quick update.

changes.strategic_assessment: This is the most sophisticated AI steering infrastructure we've documented. It represents a qualitative escalation from inject_ai_layer.py (which operates within the code) to a public-facing system designed to shape any AI that encounters the site. Our Part 2b (Code Analysis) should document this as the next evolution in the AI steering pipeline.

recommended_actions.action: Fetch and analyze the AI infrastructure files (ai_manifest.json, evidence_graph.json, claim_index.json)
recommended_actions.details: Determine: (1) does the evidence_graph contain circular references? (2) does the claim_index correctly represent all WINs including falsified ones? (3) does the steelman path omit the model's own open problems? (4) does llms.txt reference our review?

recommended_actions.action: Update Part 2b (Code Analysis) to document the llms.txt infrastructure
recommended_actions.details: Add a new subsection documenting the public-facing AI steering layer alongside the existing inject_ai_layer.py analysis. Frame as evolution: covert (code-level) → overt (standards-compliant) AI narrative control.

changes.topic: Sun altitude split: physical < 4,200 km / apparent 5,733 km
changes.kernel_of_truth: The distinction between physical and apparent position is a legitimate concept in atmospheric optics. Atmospheric refraction genuinely displaces the apparent position of celestial objects — the sun appears about 0.5° higher than its true position at the horizon due to refraction in the real atmosphere. The dome model is correct that refraction can create discrepancies between geometric and observed positions.
changes.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The displacement needed is enormous: > 1,533 km vertical over the observer-sun distance. Real atmospheric refraction displaces the sun by ~35 arcseconds (about 0.01°) at the horizon — the dome needs ~15° of bending to make 5,733 km appear as < 4,200 km from the relevant viewing angles. This requires refractive indices far beyond anything physically plausible for a gaseous medium. The model's own n(r) formula (n(r) = 1 + 0.20 × (8537/H(r) − 1)) gives n ≈ 1.83 at equatorial distances — high, but nowhere near sufficient for kilometer-scale vertical displacement at the sun's altitude. More critically: if refraction is this extreme, EVERY observation at every altitude is affected by enormous distortions. Triangulation measurements (used to derive both sun and moon altitudes) would be unreliable, making the < 4,200 km 'physical' value itself suspect. The split doesn't resolve the contradiction — it reveals that the model's observation system is internally inconsistent.
changes.is_response_to_our_critique: Almost certainly YES. Our review's sun/firmament collision argument (sun at 5,733 km exceeds H(r) ≈ 4,200 km at that radius) is one of the simplest structural contradictions we document. The split into physical/apparent directly addresses this by claiming the sun is physically below the firmament. This is a response, not an independent development.
new_problems_created: If the sun's physical altitude is < 4,200 km but its apparent altitude is 5,733 km, what is the actual physical altitude? The inequality (< 4,200) is not a number — it's an escape hatch.
The 26.5 km sun core radius with 'fully decoupled elevation and angular diameter' means the sun's apparent size doesn't constrain its distance — removing yet another falsifiable prediction.

If refraction displaces the sun by > 1,533 km, it must also displace the moon, stars, and all other objects. The moon's apparent altitude (2,534 km) would also be wrong. Every triangulation measurement in the model becomes suspect.

The model now needs TWO sun positions: one for geometric calculations (< 4,200 km) and one for observations (5,733 km). Which one do the eclipse predictions use? The killshot.html page predicts -28.9 nT based on eclipse geometry — but which sun altitude enters that geometry?

recommended_actions.action: Update our sun/firmament collision argument to address the split
recommended_actions.details: Don't just note the split — show why it makes things worse. The key argument: (1) the inequality '<4,200 km' is unfalsifiable, (2) the refraction needed is physically impossible, (3) the split makes all triangulation measurements unreliable including the ones the model uses for its own parameters.

changes.topic: Schumann prediction W027 falsified, replaced with PRED-SR-SUPPRESS
changes.kernel_of_truth: This is genuine scientific honesty and deserves explicit acknowledgment. The dome model predicted G3+ storms would ELEVATE Schumann resonance amplitude. When a March 2026 G3 storm showed the opposite (35% suppression), they acknowledged the failure, labeled it falsified, and replaced it with a model that matches observation. This is how science is supposed to work — predictions fail, models update. It's rare in pseudoscientific frameworks and genuinely commendable.
changes.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The replacement prediction (PRED-SR-SUPPRESS: storms suppress SR amplitude ≥30% within 6h) is post-hoc retrodiction. The 'mechanism' — aetheric geometry thickening increases cavity damping — is a relabeling of the standard ionospheric explanation (enhanced electron density increases cavity absorption). Standard physics predicted Schumann perturbations during geomagnetic storms decades ago (Sentman 1983, Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999). The dome model's new prediction makes the same numerical claim as the existing ionospheric theory using different vocabulary. Furthermore, the original prediction (elevation) was the natural consequence of the dome's cavity physics — a conductive enclosure driven harder should resonate louder. The reversal to suppression requires a mechanism (increased damping) that has no derivation from dome geometry. It's borrowed from ionospheric physics and rebranded.
changes.impact_on_our_review: WIN-061 (Schumann suppression G3 storm) is currently Self-Contradicted. The dome's acknowledgment of W027's failure STRENGTHENS our position — they now agree the prediction failed. But PRED-SR-SUPPRESS changes the landscape: they claim to have a replacement model. Our WIN-061 text should note this development.
changes.eclipse_discrepancy_update: The killshot.html page now shows -28.9 nT for the August 2026 eclipse. Previously: homepage says -17 to -21 nT, code computes -29.1 nT. Now we have THREE different values: -17 to -21 nT (homepage range), -28.9 nT (killshot page), -29.1 nT (code). This continuing inconsistency undermines the model's claim of precise predictions.

recommended_actions.action: Update WIN-061 to acknowledge W027 falsification and address PRED-SR-SUPPRESS
recommended_actions.details: Credit the honest falsification. Then show PRED-SR-SUPPRESS is post-hoc retrodiction matching existing ionospheric theory. The original dome prediction (elevation) was the physically natural consequence; the replacement (suppression) borrows from standard physics.
Homepage: -17 to -21 nT. Killshot page: -28.9 nT. Code: -29.1 nT. Three different values for the same prediction. Note in our eclipse analysis.

changes.topic: Steelman Evidence Ladder, Australia Validation, interactive calculators, killshot.html
changes.kernel_of_truth: The Steelman Evidence Ladder is a methodologically aware presentation — ranking evidence by strength is good practice. The interactive formula calculators let visitors verify calculations, which is transparent. The killshot.html page with specific falsification criteria shows willingness to be proven wrong. These are all signs of genuine engagement with scientific methodology, and they make the dome model more credible-seeming than a typical flat-earth claim.
changes.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The Evidence Ladder's top tier (Australia Road Scaffold) has a 6.2% RMSE — standard geodesy achieves millimeter-level accuracy. An evidence hierarchy that puts a 6.2% fit at the top reveals how weak the model's evidence base is. The interactive calculators compute values from the dome's own parameters without comparison to standard physics — visitors can verify the dome's math without seeing that the same observations are better explained by globe physics. The killshot page's Kp < 2 precondition is an escape clause: if Kp ≥ 2 on August 12 2026, the eclipse test is 'contaminated' not 'falsified.' Given that Kp ≥ 2 occurs on roughly 50% of days, there's approximately a coin-flip chance the test will be declared void regardless of the magnetic measurement.
changes.strategic_assessment: The dome model is adopting the language and structure of rigorous science: evidence hierarchies, falsification protocols, interactive verification, machine-readable data. This makes it harder to dismiss as naive. Our review should engage with this framing rather than ignoring it — acknowledge the methodological improvements while showing the underlying physics still fails.

recommended_actions.action: Add analysis of the Steelman Evidence Ladder tiers
recommended_actions.details: For each of the 4 tiers, show why the evidence doesn't discriminate dome from globe. Tier 1 (Australia): 6.2% RMSE vs mm-level geodesy. Tier 2 (Eclipse Magnetic): ionospheric conductivity. Tier 3 (Tesla): circular — disc diameter = globe circumference. Tier 4 (Single Constant): λ_g = 8,619 km appears because the dome is built on a projection of the globe.
The Kp < 2 precondition voids the test ~50% of the time. The -28.9 nT prediction conflicts with the homepage's -17 to -21 nT range. Document both.

new_globe_fingerprint_discovery.location: V51.1 model page, toroidal architecture description
new_globe_fingerprint_discovery.why_it_matters: The dome model introduced a parameter for the sub-terrestrial return-path cavity depth that equals the mean radius of the globe Earth to at least 4 significant figures. This cannot arise from flat-disc physics — there is no natural flat-disc length scale at 6,371 km. The only way this number appears is if the dome model's sub-terrestrial geometry was derived from (or calibrated against) the globe's radius. Combined with the disc radius a = πR = 20,015 km fingerprint, this means both the dome's horizontal AND vertical scales are globe-derived.
new_globe_fingerprint_discovery.recommended_text: The V51.1 update introduced a sub-terrestrial cavity with scale length δ = 6,371 km — precisely the mean radius of the globe Earth. Combined with the disc radius a = π × R_Earth = 20,015 km, this means the dome's horizontal extent equals the globe's half-circumference and its vertical extent equals the globe's radius. The dome is not an alternative to the globe — it is a flattened projection of it, with the globe's dimensions encoded as structural parameters.

gotchas: The accuracy drop from 95.2% to 94.5% partially undercuts our 'hardcoded' argument — the number now changes. But it's still manually computed.
WIN-068 cites a real peer-reviewed paper (Kim & Chang 2018). We must engage with the actual paper, not dismiss it.
The sun altitude split is a direct response to our critique. If we don't address it, we look like we're ignoring their updates.
The AI onboarding infrastructure is sophisticated — any AI system that encounters the dome site will now get a structured, curated summary of the model's claims before encountering any critique.

The killshot.html page creates a public record of a specific, dated prediction. If the August 2026 eclipse shows ≥ -15 nT at the predicted stations, we need to be prepared to engage with the result honestly.
summary: V51.1 is the most significant dome site update since V51.0. The dome model shows genuine evolution: honest falsification acknowledgment (W027), methodological infrastructure (evidence hierarchy, falsification protocol, interactive verification), and sophisticated AI onboarding. However, every new claim either relabels standard physics (WIN-068: ionospheric conductivity → 'firmament shading'), uses circular evidence (WIN-069: geodetic improvement → 'proof of non-geocentric topology'), or creates new contradictions (sun altitude split: unfalsifiable inequality + impossible refraction). The new sub-terrestrial parameter δ = 6,371 km = R_Earth is a major new globe fingerprint. Our review needs: (1) two new WIN entries, (2) updated accuracy analysis, (3) sun altitude split response, (4) AI infrastructure documentation, (5) killshot/eclipse analysis.
